Ad Text:
We've been exclusively retained to partner with a cutting-edge technology business building a genuinely impactful enterprise AI platform already operating at scale.

This is a rare opportunity to join a small, high-calibre team where your work will directly shape a product used by real customers, not just prototypes.

If you're someone who thrives at the intersection of full-stack engineering and applied AI, and you enjoy owning outcomes end to end, this role offers both autonomy and technical depth in equal measure.

Why this opportunity stands out

You'll be joining a product-led environment where AI is not a side experiment but the core of the platform. The business has already integrated multiple LLM providers, deployed hundreds of production functions, and built a sophisticated multi-tenant SaaS platform.

This is a chance to work on meaningful problems like document intelligence, knowledge systems, and workflow automation while pushing the boundaries of how AI is used in production.

What you'll be doing

You'll operate across the full stack, taking ideas from concept through to production. Your work will include

Designing and building LLM-powered features including prompt engineering, agentic workflows, and RAG pipelines
Developing modern React applications with rich UI components, data visualisation, and document generation capabilities
Building and scaling backend services using Node.js, TypeScript, and serverless architecture
Extending integrations with third-party platforms such as Microsoft and Google ecosystems
Enhancing document processing pipelines including embeddings, chunking, and semantic retrieval
Managing and optimising PostgreSQL databases including vector search and performance tuning
Contributing to cloud infrastructure, CI/CD, and system observability
Delivering secure, enterprise-grade features including SSO, RBAC, and audit logging
What we're looking for

This role is suited to a senior engineer who has already shipped AI-enabled products into real-world environments.
You'll bring

Strong full-stack experience building and delivering web applications end to end
Hands-on experience with generative AI in production, not just experimentation
Deep capability in React and TypeScript across modern frontend architecture
Solid backend engineering skills with Node.js and API design
Experience working with LLM APIs and building intelligent workflows
Strong understanding of RAG pipelines, embeddings, and retrieval strategies
Experience with cloud platforms, ideally Azure or equivalent
Confidence operating autonomously and owning complex features from start to finish
Highly regarded experience
Multi-model AI routing and optimisation across cost, speed, and quality
Rich text editing frameworks and complex UI interactions
Vector databases and hybrid search techniques
Multi-tenant SaaS architecture and enterprise security models
Experience in fast-moving startup or scale-up environments
What's on offer
High ownership and direct impact on product direction
Work alongside a small, highly capable team solving complex AI problems
Flexibility to work remotely within Australia or from Melbourne
Exposure to modern AI tooling, infrastructure, and large-scale systems
A role where speed, innovation, and quality are genuinely valued
If you're looking to step into a role where AI is core to the product and you can truly influence how it evolves, this is one worth exploring.

Engineering the Future of Connectivity

 

At NetComm, we’ve been connecting the world for over 40 years - designing and building innovative broadband products that connect homes, businesses, and industries across the globe. From Fixed Wireless to Fibre Extension and Home Broadband, our technology keeps people connected where it matters most.

 

Our commitment to innovation has made us pioneers in Fixed Wireless Access, creating the world's first 5G self-install solution and revolutionizing how carriers deploy broadband services. 

 

Our mission is to listen to our customers and apply bold, innovative thinking to solve complex problems and connect the world.

 

We’re looking for a motivated Manufacturing Test Automation Engineer to lead the design, development and maintenance of our product manufacturing test systems.

 

 

WHAT WILL BE YOUR FOCUS?

 

·       Lead the design, development and maintenance of production test systems.

·       Collaborate cross-functionally with Hardware, Software and Project leads to define test strategies and to develop comprehensive manufacturing test requirements.

·       Develop RESTful APIs and Python libraries to interface with test equipment, devices under test (DUTs), and manufacturing execution system (MES).

·       Design and implement automated test scripts and applications using Python and/or C/C++ for functional and system-level testing.

·       Deploy and maintain automated test systems (ATS) across Contract Manufacturer/3PL ensuring proper training, quality, efficiency, and cost optimization.

·       Apply automation best practices such as containerization and utilizing AI tools to streamline development and deployment workflows efficiently.

·       Support enhancement and maintenance of proprietary full-stack web application for production data management, enabling efficient manufacturing and logistics operations.

 

 

WHAT DO WE LOOK FOR IN YOU?

 

Experience: Telecommunications and/or electronics manufacturing industry.

Technical Expertise: Python, MySQL, Docker, Git, REST APIs.

Technical Know-how: Familiarity with manufacturing test systems, hardware/software integrations, and AI-assisted development tools.

Software Knowledge: Strong understanding of software development principles, including software architecture, design patterns, and testing methodologies.

Ownership: Sense of responsibility to deliver and follow through.

Critical Thinking: Analytical and problem-solving skills.

Teamwork: Ability to thrive in fast-paced cross-functional teams.

 

 

WHAT'S IN IT FOR YOU? 

·       Inclusive and collaborative working environment.

·       Flexible working arrangements and hybrid WFH model.

·       Exposure to innovative technology.

·       Professional development and training opportunities.

·       Regular wellbeing and diversity focused initiatives.

·       Various perks, including company-sponsored insurance, recognition awards and others to look forward to.

 

 

We believe in the value of a vibrant, diverse and inclusive workforce where our team members feel valued, respected and empowered to be bold, innovative and create strong connections with the world.

 

We embrace differences and we welcome applications from people of all backgrounds including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, people with disabilities and other diversity groups.

Salary: Attractive salary - work close to home and WFH
Ad Text:
Join the Construction Revolution: Data Engineer (Microsoft Fabric)
Ready to build something from the ground up? We aren't just talking about infrastructure - we’re talking about the future of our data.

The Opportunity
As the Data Engineer at SEE Group, you won't just be managing a legacy system; you will be the primary architect and builder of our brand-new Enterprise Data Warehouse. This is a rare, "greenfield" opportunity to transition our entire data estate into Microsoft Fabric, unifying insights across our entities - SEE Civil, SEE Mining, SEE Fleet, and Specialised Aggregates - into a single source of truth.


This role is different! 

Total Ownership: You are the architect. From deploying the Microsoft Fabric environment to designing the OneLake data structures, you own the build.

Cutting-Edge Tech: Forget outdated ETL. You’ll be working with a modern Medallion architecture (Bronze/Silver/Gold) and optimizing for real-time visibility through Direct Lake connectivity.

The "SEE Way": We aren’t just a corporate office. We value a "Boots and All" attitude where we pitch in, own our actions, and solve problems by embracing new ideas.

Tangible Impact: Your work directly enables real-time visibility into project costs, fleet health, and operational safety.

What You’ll Do

Build the Foundation: Deploy and manage the Fabric environment, including workspace governance and capacity monitoring.

Engineer the Pipelines: Create robust ingestion pipelines using Fabric Data Factory and develop Spark-based transformation logic using Fabric Notebooks.

Create Unity: Implement Shortcuts to link disparate data sources (Azure, AWS, On-prem) without unnecessary duplication.

Collaborate: Partner with the Business Systems Manager and site-based Managers to translate heavy industry operations into actionable KPIs.

Who You Are

The Fabric Expert: You have hands-on experience with Lakehouses, Warehouses, and OneLake.

A Technical Powerhouse: You’re an expert in SQL and proficient in PySpark for data engineering.

A Proven Builder: You’ve delivered at least one end-to-end Data Warehouse project and have a deep understanding of Star Schema and Kimball methodologies.

Mobile & Ready: You have at least 5 years of experience, a current Driver’s License, and a willingness to travel to our various sites to see the data in action.

The SEE Way
SEE Group is a family-built company with over 35 years of experience in delivering excellence across civil construction, mining, and infrastructure. At SEE Group, we don’t just build projects - we build careers.

Be part of a down-to-earth, supportive team that values diversity and inclusion

Benefit from career growth and tailored development opportunities

Enjoy personalised rewards and recognition for your hard work

Work on impactful projects that shape communities

Collaborate with passionate people who bring a "boots & all" attitude to everything they do

Are you ready to own it?
If you are a Data Engineer who thrives in a "new role, new environment" setting and would like to play a pivotal role in the digital transformation of a leading civil construction and mining group, we want to hear from you.

If you would like to join our team and hold full, unlimited working rights in Australia, we encourage you to submit your CV by clicking apply now.
As an Equal Opportunity Employer, Women and People of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Island heritage are strongly encouraged to apply.
Interested to learn more about SEE Group? Visit our website at www.seegroup.com.au

About the role

As Principal Solutions Architect, you will set the architectural direction and provide strategic technical leadership for the Department’s operational tooling ecosystem, with a strong focus on ServiceNow and its integrations.

You will design and guide solutions that convert high volume‑, multisource telemetry into meaningful, actionable intelligence. This enables ‑Multivendor Environment (MVE) support teams to rapidly triage and resolve incidents, while maintaining an immutable, auditable source of truth to assure service performance and provider accountability‑.

The role plays a critical part in uplifting SIAM maturity, using data driven‑ insights to improve service outcomes across complex, highly regulated government environments.

Skills & Experience

Deep expertise in ServiceNow ITSM, including Event Management, Service Mapping and Advanced Performance Analytics.

Proven delivery of advanced analytics and reporting using ServiceNow and Power BI, producing executive, operational and practice level‑ insights.

Strong experience integrating ServiceNow with ScienceLogic (SL1), Splunk, and other operations platforms, with a focus on measuring and improving tool effectiveness.

Familiarity with modern monitoring and observability tools such as Prometheus, Splunk, ScienceLogic, Aternity, and cloud native‑ monitoring solutions.

Demonstrated success operating within Australian Government frameworks, managing complex systems with strict security, risk and compliance controls.

Experience supporting Multivendor‑ Environments (MVE) where disparate vendor tools must align to deliver end-to-end‑ services.

Ability to uplift SIAM practices through evidence based insights, metrics‑ and continuous improvement initiatives.

Must hold and maintain an NV1 Australian Government Security Clearance.

 Close off date

This role is being recruited with urgency. Applications close Monday night, 6 April 2026.

Our Company

NEC brings over a century of global innovation and more than 50 years of Australian expertise, delivering advanced ICT and anything‑as‑a‑service solutions. With a strong focus on Public Safety, NEC partners with government and enterprise to solve complex challenges, improve service delivery and help build a safer society.

NEC is committed to diversity and inclusion and strongly encourages applications from people with disability, Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, and candidates from culturally and linguistically diverse backgrounds.

Become part of OneNEC. Apply online today!

About us

Silicon Quantum Computing is pioneering the future of quantum technology. As the world’s first company to manufacture qubits with atomic precision, we’re delivering quantum advantage today, not decades from now.

Founded by Professor Michelle Simmons, one of the world’s leading materials scientists, SQC has achieved major breakthroughs including the first single-atom transistor and the first integrated circuit built with atomic precision. Our Chair, Simon Segars, former CEO of ARM, brings deep global experience as we scale our technology and commercial impact.

Our quantum machine learning chip, Watermelon, is already accelerating AI in ways classical systems can’t match. We’re generating significant revenue and working closely with multinational partners to solve complex problems with quantum solutions.

SQC is a fast-paced, high-accountability environment where people grow quickly and contribute meaningfully. If you’re excited by deep tech, ambitious about your career, and ready to help shape the future of computing, we’d love to hear from you.

About the role

Working at the cutting edge of semiconductor device fabrication for atomic-scale devices alongside global experts at SQC and UNSW Sydney, you’ll contribute to the fabrication and characterisation of quantum devices in a cleanroom environment, helping scale up to utility-scale logical quantum computing.

This role reports to the Quantum Device Fabrication and Integration Team Lead and will work closely with cleanroom managers, scientists, and engineers.

Role responsibilities

Perform semiconductor device fabrication using techniques such as electron beam lithography, dry and wet etching, and metal deposition

Assist in maintaining cleanroom equipment and managing consumables

Record and analyse process data to support device optimisation

Contribute to process documentation and safe work procedures

Provide technical support and training to research staff and students

Collaborate with scientists, engineers, and cleanroom managers to ensure high device yield and operational continuity

Your experience

Undergraduate or Master’s degree in Engineering, Physics, Nanotechnology, or a related field

1–3 years’ experience in cleanroom semiconductor processing (industry or academic)

Hands-on experience with one or more of: electron beam lithography, etching, metal deposition

Strong attention to detail and ability to follow protocols and safety procedures

The recruitment process

For roles at SQC, expect 3-4 interviews, meeting a few members of the team and focusing on your core eligibility for the role, your skills and how they align, and finally your values and how they align. We'll give you a more specific runthrough if you're successful in making it to the first interview.

As part of our obligations to our clients, we require that successful candidates submit to background checks, including a National Police Check, Right to Work checks, as well as employment and qualifications verification. We won't contact anyone until we're confident there's a fit between SQC and yourself.

How to apply

The best and simplest way is to apply directly here, rather than messaging anyone on the SQC team directly. If you have any questions that you'd like answered before committing to applying, please feel free to email us at careers@sqc.com.au.


Please note

This position may require access to export-controlled information (ECI) subject to EAR and ITAR. Final employment decision is contingent upon satisfactory completion of export control screenings and obtaining any necessary export licenses or approvals, based on nationality, citizenship, and other factors considered by applicable export control regulations.

About Civillo

Civillo is a Melbourne-born SaaS platform purpose-built for the civil construction industry. We bring together spatial data, design files, permits, and field updates into one map-driven environment, helping engineers, surveyors, and project managers deliver some of Australia's largest infrastructure projects more safely and efficiently.

We're a close-knit team who care deeply about the quality of what we build and the real-world impact it has on complex, high-stakes construction sites across the country.




The Role

We're looking for a mid-level Software Engineer to join our product engineering team. You'll work primarily on our C# / .NET backend and Vue frontend, contributing to a platform that processes and visualises complex geospatial data and workflows for some of Australia's biggest infrastructure projects.

This is a role for someone who takes pride in well-crafted backend code, enjoys working across the stack, and is excited by the prospect of helping shape a platform that's actively evolving.




What You'll Be Doing

Collaborating with product, design, and domain experts to translate user needs into well-crafted software

Designing and shipping features across our C# / .NET backend and Vue frontend

Contributing to CI/CD pipelines and cloud infrastructure on AWS

Participating in code reviews and helping lift team standards

Working on performance, reliability, and scalability as our platform grows




What We're Looking For

Must-haves

2–5 years of professional software development experience

Solid proficiency in C#, .NET, and JavaScript.

Comfortable working across the stack; you don't need to be a specialist in every layer, but you can pick things up and contribute end to end

Strong fundamentals: clean code, testing, and version control (Git)

Familiarity with Cloud services and CI/CD workflows (e.g. Jenkins)

Nice to haves

Experience with Vue or another modern JS framework (React, Angular)

Familiarity with Node.js, Docker

Experience in a SaaS product environment

Any experience with C++

Exposure to geospatial concepts: GIS, CAD file formats, coordinate systems, or mapping libraries




What's On Offer

$100,000 – $130,000 + superannuation, depending on experience

Flexible hybrid working, structured around your skills and the needs of the product

Professional development. Civillo actively supports ongoing learning and encourages all team members to undertake continuing professional development. Speak to us about what that looks like in practice.

A genuinely interesting domain: geospatial software powering real infrastructure across Australia

Small team with direct impact. Your work ships and people use it.




How to Apply

A cover letter is welcome but not required. We're more interested in what you've built than how you write about yourself. If you have a GitHub profile or examples of your work you'd like to share, we'd love to see them - but again they're not required.

Candidates must be Australian permanent residents or citizens with full working rights.

Ad Text:
Join us at a pivotal moment of growth

Box Hill Institute is entering a significant period of growth and transformation, with major investment underway across our IT, Digital and AI implementation space.

We're building capability, embedding smarter ways of working, and leveraging emerging technologies to support our people, students and future workforce.

APPLY NOW and be part of what's next.

About the Role


Supporting the Technology That Powers Education

Play a hands-on role in keeping the technology that supports teaching and business operations running reliably every day. As a Systems Engineer, you will support the stability, security and performance of Box Hill Institute's core data centre and hybrid infrastructure. Working across on premise and cloud connected platforms, this role provides practical operational support across compute, storage, virtualisation and enterprise backup and recovery environments, including Veeam. You will be part of the IT Infrastructure Services team, supporting a hybrid environment that spans traditional data centre technologies and platforms such as Microsoft Azure and Microsoft Entra ID, while working closely with internal teams and external vendors across a multi-campus environment.

What you'll be responsible for

Operate, maintain and optimise on-premise compute, storage, HCI and virtualisation platforms to ensure health, capacity, availability and performance
Support hybrid infrastructure environments, integrating on-premise platforms with cloud-connected services including Microsoft Azure
Administer enterprise backup and recovery platforms, including Veeam, performing verification, restore testing and disaster recovery readiness
Support hybrid identity platforms, including Active Directory and Microsoft Entra ID, monitoring platform health, authentication dependencies and integrations
Provide proactive infrastructure monitoring and remediation, technical support for incidents and changes, maintain asset and lifecycle records, develop documentation and collaborate with vendors and internal teams

What you'll bring

Experience supporting enterprise data centre and hybrid infrastructure environments
Hands-on capability across compute, storage, virtualisation and enterprise backup platforms
Experience administering backup and recovery solutions, including Veeam or equivalent
Working knowledge of Microsoft Azure infrastructure and hybrid identity platforms including Active Directory and Microsoft Entra ID
Strong troubleshooting, documentation, analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to resolve infrastructure performance and availability issues in cross-functional teams

Qualifications / Requirements

Advanced Diploma or Degree in IT, Computer Science or equivalent experience, with certifications or equivalent experience across Azure, identity platforms, virtualisation, backup technologies and ITIL
Demonstrated experience supporting enterprise data centre environments including physical and virtual servers, enterprise storage and hyperconverged infrastructure across multi-site or distributed environments
Hands-on capability in backup, recovery and resilience practices, infrastructure monitoring, lifecycle management, technical documentation and working with vendors or managed service providers

Why work with us?

At Box Hill Institute, we offer a supportive and rewarding work environment with benefits that are designed to help you thrive both professionally and personally:

Flexible work arrangements
On-site parking
Generous leave options such as Emergency response leave and up to 16 weeks paid Parental Leave
Health & wellbeing supports and resources, including FREE flu-vaccinations, FREE skin checks, FREE health checks and corporate health insurance discounts
Financial perks like novated leasing and salary packaging
Unique campus experiences to support student learning
Exclusive staff discounts on CAE/Box Hill Institute short courses for staff and their immediate family

APPLY NOW

All applications must be submitted online and include a cover letter and current resume.

For specific enquiries relating to this position, please contact us via email at

it-careers@boxhill.edu.au

Applications close: 5:00pm, 24th April 2026

We are a 2025 Circle Back Initiative Employer - we commit to respond to every applicant.

Box Hill Institute is a welcoming, safe and respectful work environment. We are work-life friendly and an equal opportunity employer. We encourage people from all backgrounds, abilities and identities to apply for our vacancies.

Box Hill Institute is a Child Safe Organisation and has a zero tolerance towards the harm or abuse of children and young people. We believe that child safety is everyone's responsibility. All employees, volunteers, contractors and service providers are required to promote a culture of child safety, comply with the Child Safe Standards and other relevant legislation and reporting obligations of suspected child abuse as per the Institute's Child Safety Policy and Procedures. All employees are required to hold and maintain a current, valid Working with Children Check for the duration of their employment at their own expense.

Candidates for employment at Box Hill Institute will be subject to pre-employment probity checks, and must satisfy Box Hill Institute's pre-conditions for employment.

Box Hill Institute reserves the right to withdraw an advertised position at any stage.

About the role

Working as Data Architect your role will be responsible for designing and evolving the organisation’s data architecture posture to enable secure, high‑quality and trusted data across analytics, operations and digital products.

You will ensure data models, platforms and integration patterns align with enterprise architecture, business outcomes and regulatory obligations, while governing data quality, security and lifecycle management practices.

Key responsibilities of this role are:

Define and maintain data architecture roadmaps and specification standards leveraging Data Flow Diagrams, Entity Relationship Diagrams, Data Definitions and semantics.
Design solution data architectures for programs and platforms assuring delivery quality and conformance to data architecture.
Establish and embed data governance and lifecycle practices and assure data security, privacy, and compliance by design.
Model enterprise data, evaluate schematics, and drive semantic consistency.
Consult, influence, and provide specialist advice around emerging technologies and evolving patterns.

 

NHVR’s Data & Technology team are currently undertaking growth and considerable transformation, and this is an exciting time to join the organisation.

To learn more, the position description can be found on the NHVR Careers site - https://careers.nhvr.gov.au/jobs/search

 

About You

To be successful in this role you will ideally possess tertiary qualifications or demonstrate demonstrated (7-10 years) experience in data architecture solution architecture, or data engineering with substantial modelling and governance exposure.

Your toolkit for success:

Experience across data platforms (e.g., warehouses, lakes, streaming) and integration patterns.
Exposure to multiple, diverse technologies, platforms, and processing environments including on-prem and cloud.
Understanding of strategic and emerging technology trends, and the practical application of existing and emerging technologies to new and evolving business and operating models.
Understanding of product management, agile principles and development methodologies and capability of supporting agile teams by providing advice and guidance on opportunities, impact, and risks, taking account of technical and architectural debt.
Experience developing data architecture patterns, strategies, and roadmaps.

Why join us?

The National Heavy Vehicle Regulator (NHVR) commenced operations in 2013 as the national entity responsible for regulating all vehicles in Australia over 4.5 tonnes gross vehicle mass. The NHVR minimises the compliance burden on the heavy vehicle transport industry, reduces duplication and inconsistences across state and territory borders, and ultimately for the heavy vehicle business with government in Australia.

We are headquartered in Brisbane and employ more than 1000 people across the ACT, New South Wales, Queensland, South Australia, Tasmania and Victoria.

We offer-

A competitive salary package from $161,808.27 + superannuation
Monthly RDO and paid leave over Christmas closure.
Hybrid working environment – up to 2 days from home.
Up to15.25% superannuation through a co-contribution scheme
Leave loading 17.5%
Wellbeing programs – discounted fitness through Fitness Passport, Discount health insurance, flu vaccine, financial programs including discounted banking products, finance information sessions.
Salary packaging options including novated lease.
Discounted IT products including Microsoft and free family password security plan.
Ongoing training and professional development support
Professional membership

 

Application Process

Please apply with your CV and cover letter addressing the essential requirements of the role.

Applications close: 5.00pm 16th April 2026 (applications may close earlier than the listed closing date if suitable candidate/s are identified prior).

If you have any questions, please don’t hesitate to contact the NHVR recruitment team on recruitment@nhvr.gov.au.

Successful applicants will be required to complete standard pre-employment checks including a national police and work rights check.

Please Note: We will not require any support from Recruitment Agencies at this time and kindly request no contact is made. Only direct applications will be considered.

We are committed to being an inclusive, diverse and flexible workplace where differences are valued. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, experience and abilities.

DingGo is on a mission to modernise how Australia’s automotive ecosystem handles vehicle damage and repair — from first notice of loss through to repair completion. Our market leading data-driven platform serves panelbeaters, fleet operators, and insurers with tools that streamline the end-to-end crash management process through workflow automation and crash intelligence that helps fleets, insurers, and repairers make smarter, faster decisions.

We’re a fast-growing, product-led company with deep domain expertise and ambitious plans for AU/NZ and beyond.

We’re looking for a hands-on Lead ML Engineer to join our growing team. This is a pivotal, high-ownership role that sits at the intersection of AI and Product — you’ll be responsible for the full lifecycle of our AI/ML capabilities, from architecture and experimentation through to production deployment and ongoing monitoring.

Your work will directly power DingGo’s suite of AI products: damage detection, repair cost estimation, document intelligence, and more. You’ll work closely with our Head of Data, AI & New Products , data scientists, and product squads to ship real AI features into the hands of real customers.

We value curiosity, rigour, and a bias for impact. If you want to build AI that actually matters— that gets used by real businesses every day — this is the role for you.


Relevant Skills & Technologies

AI/ML Engineering · Computer Vision · Document AI (DocAI) · Large Language Models (LLMs) · MLOps · Data Science · Python · AWS (Lambda, S3, ECS, CloudWatch, Fargate) · Snowflake · Docker · MLflow · BitBucket / Git · FastAPI / REST APIs · SQL


Required Qualification and Experience

• A Bachelor’s degree or higher qualification required

• Minimum two years of hands-on ML/AI engineering experience, including production deployments

• Strong Python engineering skills: clean, testable, maintainable code

• Proven computer vision experience — image classification, object detection, damage assessment, or similar

• Experience working with LLMs in production: prompt engineering, evaluation frameworks, guardrails, and cost management

• Document AI / unstructured data processing experience (PDFs, forms, images of documents)

• Practical MLOps experience: model versioning, inference pipelines, orchestration, and monitoring (MLflow or equivalent)

• Strong AWS experience: Lambda, ECS, S3, CloudWatch; familiarity with serverless and containerised deployments

• Solid SQL and data skills; experience with Snowflake or a comparable cloud data warehouse

• Experience designing and building REST APIs and backend services in Python (e.g. FastAPI)

• Ability to define and own AI solution architecture, not just implement tasks

• Comfortable working in an early-stage, fast-moving product environment with shifting priorities

Key Responsibilities

AI/ML Model Development & Evaluation

• Design, develop, train, and fine-tune computer vision and multimodal ML models

• Build and maintain LLM-powered pipelines for document intelligence, repair estimation, and structured data extraction

• Develop rigorous evaluation frameworks for model quality, regression testing, and performance benchmarking

• Implement model observability and drift monitoring to maintain quality in production

• Conduct AI R&D: experiment with new architectures, techniques, and open-source models to advance DingGo’s capabilities

LLM Engineering

• Define the target prompt architecture for LLM-powered features

• Build and maintain guardrails, safety layers, and output validation for LLM features in customer-facing products

• Evaluate and benchmark LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, AWS Bedrock, open- source) to optimise quality and cost

• Manage LLM usage costs and ensure efficient, responsible use of inference compute AI Solution Architecture

• Define and own DingGo’s target-state AI/ML stack, selecting tools, frameworks, and infrastructure components that balance capability, cost, and operational complexity

• Design the optimal architecture for each AI feature: real-time vs. batch, synchronous vs. async, cloud-managed vs. self-hosted

• Evaluate open-source tools (Hugging Face, LangChain, LlamaIndex, MLflow, Ray, etc.) and AWS-native services to build a pragmatic, scalable stack

AI Product Development

• Collaborate closely with Product and Data teams to translate product requirements into AI/ML feature designs

• Embed AI capabilities within DingGo’s SaaS platform, including new standalone AI products for fleets, insurers, and repairers

• Contribute to sprint planning, technical scoping, and delivery estimates for AI features

• Ensure AI features are explainable, auditable, and meet the expectations of enterprise and insurance clients

MLOps & Production Deployment

• Build and maintain CI/CD pipelines for ML models using BitBucket Pipelines, Docker, and AWS (ECS, Lambda)

• Work with DevOps to deploy and scale AI services in production, with clear runbooks and rollback procedures

• Maintain MLflow (or equivalent) for experiment tracking, model registry, and deployment artefacts

• Manage containerised model serving and inference endpoints optimised for latency and throughput

Monitoring, Security & Cost Management

• Implement production monitoring for model performance, data quality, and system health using AWS CloudWatch and custom dashboards

• Ensure AI systems meet DingGo’s ISO 27001 compliance requirements, including data handling, access controls, and audit logging

• Proactively manage total cost of ownership (TCO) for AI/ML infrastructure, identifying and acting on cost optimisation opportunities

• Maintain high availability SLAs for customer-facing AI features

What Success Looks Like at 12 Months

• Multiple new AI products shipped and live with paying clients across fleet, insurance, and repairer segments

• DingGo’s target-state AI/ML stack is defined, documented, and stood up — providing a clear foundation for the next 2–3 years of product growth

• Model quality metrics (accuracy, precision, recall, latency) are tracked, visible, and consistently improving across core computer vision and LLM features

• A robust MLOps practice in place: repeatable deployment pipelines, model versioning, automated evals, and monitoring from day one

• ISO 27001 compliance maintained across all AI systems, with clear data handling policies and audit trails

• AI infrastructure costs are tracked and optimised, with a clear view of per-product and per-client unit economics

• You’re a trusted technical voice within the business — influencing product direction, architectural decisions, and the team’s engineering culture

Salary: Up to $85k inc super p.a. ot day-rate option
Ad Text:
Associate Data Engineer - AWS & Python

Leading banking client (consulting through FinXL)

FinXL is seeking an Associate Data Engineer - AWS & Python to join our consulting team and be deployed with a leading banking client on a 6 months contract in Sydney.

3+ years of proven experience working on Data Integration projects.
Strong experience working with core AWS services.
Proficiency in Python for data manipulation and integration tasks.
Hands-on experience with SQL databases.

6 months contract with a leading banking client.
Hybrid working model with 3 days/ week in the office in Sydney CBD.

Next Steps

Apply now highlighting relevant skills and experience and address your application to Julia Leung. Due to the high volume of applications only shortlisted candidates can be contacted.

Job Title: Senior Automation Engineer | Identity Platform | Finance
Ad Text:
Our client's Directory Services team manages a large-scale hybrid identity platform and they need an engineer who writes the code that makes it run itself.

The identity context can be learned. The automation fundamentals can't. If you're strong in Terraform and Python and you solve problems with code, this role is worth a conversation.

What you'll be doing:

You'll be building and maintaining the automation engines that underpin a global identity platform. That means writing code (Python, PowerShell, Terraform) to automate identity lifecycle workflows, deploying and managing infrastructure as code, and building monitoring and reporting solutions to keep the platform healthy and secure. You'll work across a hybrid environment touching Active Directory, Microsoft Entra ID, Ping and Google Identity.

What they're looking for:

Strong hands-on Terraform skills
Python proficiency, comfortable building real automation logic, not just scripts
An engineering and developer mindset, you solve problems with code
Experience in cloud or hybrid infrastructure environments
Exposure to identity or access management concepts is a genuine advantage (Entra ID, AD, JML workflows)
Monitoring and reporting experience (Sentinel, Splunk, PowerBI, KQL) valued

Why this role:

Genuine build and engineering scope, not a BAU admin role
Hybrid working in Sydney
Platform that operates at global scale across a critical financial institution
A team that values engineers who build, not just manage

Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples are encouraged to apply.

Job Title: AI Engineer - Manager - AI GTM Engine Room
Ad Text:
Company description:



At EY, we're all in to shape your future with confidence. We'll help you succeed in a globally connected powerhouse of diverse teams and take your career wherever you want it to go. Join EY and help to build a better working world.



Job description:

Join EY's AI Go to Market (GTM) Engine Room, a high-velocity build capability at the centre of our Oceania AI growth strategy.

As an AI Engineer, you will lead the experimentation, prototyping and deployment of advanced AI and Generative AI solutions that support client pursuits, industry demonstrations and scalable sector offerings. Working with leading foundation models and orchestration frameworks, you will translate emerging capabilities into practical, commercially viable solutions.

Within the AI Labs Engine Room, you will operate across enterprise-grade environments, advanced tooling and significant model and compute capacity, enabling experimentation and iteration at scale within secure enterprise guardrails.

You will combine rapid innovation with production discipline, leading engineers from concept to working solution at pace, shaping reusable AI accelerators and maturing successful prototypes into scalable enterprise deployments.

You will play a key role in shaping AI positioning across priority sectors and strategic accounts.

This role can be performed from any of our EY offices across Australia. Our roles can potentially be adjusted to work flexibly with reduced hours. Please speak with us about potential options.

Your key responsibilities

Lead the prototyping and deployment of AI and Generative AI solutions supporting client pursuits and scalable sector offerings.
Architect and implement LLM-powered applications and agentic workflows, including multi-agent orchestration and advanced tool/function calling patterns.
Design and oversee data-driven architectures across cloud and hybrid environments.
Engineer scalable AI systems with embedded Responsible AI, governance and security controls.
Establish and embed MLOps and LLMOps practices including lifecycle management, experimentation tracking (MLflow, Weights & Biases), monitoring and safe deployment.
Design and optimise data pipelines integrating structured and unstructured data sources.

Mentor and guide junior engineers within the Engine Room.
Evaluate emerging models, orchestration approaches and low-code platforms to continuously strengthen EY's AI capability.

Skills and attributes for success

You are an experienced hands-on AI engineer who thrives in fast-moving environments and is comfortable leading technical design and delivery.

You will bring:

Strong foundation in AI, machine learning and Generative AI with proven hands-on delivery experience.
Deep experience working with LLMs (OpenAI, Anthropic, Llama or equivalent).
Strong experience with orchestration frameworks such as LangChain and agentic system design.
Demonstrated experience applying MLOps and LLMOps in production or near-production environments.
Strong data engineering capability and architectural decision-making experience.
Experience deploying and optimising AI workloads across AWS, Azure or GCP.
Advanced Python programming capability and strong software engineering discipline.
Strong understanding of Responsible AI, compliance and enterprise security guardrails.
Ability to lead technical discussions with architects, sector leaders and clients.

Experience leading AI solutions that support commercial pursuits or reusable sector offerings.
Experience mentoring engineers or leading technical workstreams.
Exposure to model evaluation frameworks and structured experimentation environments.
A degree in computer science, engineering, data science or related discipline.

At EY, we'll fuel you and your extraordinary talents in a diverse and inclusive culture of globally connected teams. Learn more.

A comprehensive benefits package: From a yearly wellness incentive, to access to additional 8 weeks of flex leave per year, and family-friendly policies, including 26 weeks of gender-neutral paid parental leave, we cater to your diverse needs to help you thrive both personally and professionally www.ey.com/au/benefits    
Salary: We offer a competitive salary which is open to negotiation pending on skills and experience.

Acknowledgement of Country

EY acknowledges the Traditional Owners and Custodians of the lands on which EY offices are located around Australia. We pay our respects to their cultures, and to their Elders — past, present, and emerging. Find out more about our vision for reconciliation at ey.com/en_au/careers/indigenous

Experience with production-grade AI agents for Data Science or MLOps

We work hard and play hard, so along with a competitive salary and generous performance-based bonus for permanent staff members, here’s some of our other perks:

25 days annual leave (that’s right, an additional week’s leave!)
Hybrid working policy (Monday and Friday from home) and a $1,050 home office allowance for new starters.
Industry leading Parental Leave support program with 26 weeks paid leave for Primary Carers and no wait time. We also have a progressive return to work benefit for your first 6 months back: come back for 3 days, get paid for 4; come back for 4 days, get paid for 5.

Our Focus on Diversity, Equity & Inclusion

We don’t just support diversity – we celebrate it! This is a workplace where you can thrive and feel safe to bring your whole authentic self to work.  

What’s the Green Tick Feeling?  

Well, it’s different for everyone, yet unites us all. It’s the feeling you get when you succeed, no matter what success looks like to you. It's all the little and big things that make you feel like you’re thriving when working at Sportsbet.   
   
We’re an inclusive employer who welcomes you for who you are, as you are – so, if you require adjustments to the recruitment process, please let us know in your application. We also know flex means different things to different people, so let us know how we can support you to be your best.   

If you think you’d be a great fit but don’t necessarily meet everything in the job description, please still get in touch.

You must possess Australian/NZ citizenship or Australian PR to be considered for this role.

For further details, contact Fortunato (Forty) Galluzzo on 043 870 9417 or alternately email on forty@taskgroup.com.au.

Purpose of the role

We’re building a next‑generation, automation‑first Security Operations Centre, and this role is central to making that real.

As a Cyber Detection & Automation Engineer, you’ll be hands‑on designing, building and tuning detection rules and automated response playbooks that improve how threats are identified and handled. This role focuses on doing the work — engineering high‑quality detections, reducing noise, and removing manual effort from day‑to‑day security operations.

You’ll work closely with senior engineers and analysts, contributing directly to a SOC capability that’s being built from the ground up rather than constrained by legacy processes.

What will your key responsibilities include?

Design, build, test and deploy detection rules across endpoint, network, cloud and identity data sources

Build and maintain SOAR playbooks that automate enrichment, correlation, containment and response

Continuously tune and optimise detection rules to reduce false positives and improve signal‑to‑noise

Translate threat intelligence and emerging risks into actionable detection logic

Conduct hypothesis‑driven threat hunting and convert findings into production detections

Support incident response through automated forensic collection and response actions

Build automated detection rules for exploitation attempts against known and emerging vulnerabilities

Contribute to SOC uplift initiatives, including automation and platform integration

Build automated monitoring for vulnerabilities, exposure and patch compliance

Maintain documentation for detection rules, automation and integrations

What are we looking for?

You enjoy getting stuck in, solving problems and building things that work. You’re curious by nature, comfortable learning as you go, and motivated by improving how security operations are done — not just reacting to alerts. 

We’re especially keen to hear from people with:

 Demonstrated experience in SOC operations or detection engineering

Experience writing detection rules using SIEM query languages (KQL, SPL, XQL, YARA-L)

Experience building SOAR playbooks and automation workflows

Understanding of security telemetry across cloud, endpoint, network, and identity

Familiarity with MITRE ATT&CK, NIST CSF, or Essential 8 frameworks

Scripting skills (Python, PowerShell, or similar)

Analytical, automation-focused, and collaborative mindset

What can you expect from us?

Our benefits are designed to help you thrive — in and out of work. Think health and wellbeing support like Fitness Passport, corporate health insurance and annual flu vaccinations, plus lifestyle perks including hotel discounts and flexible work arrangements. We also back what matters most with 18 weeks paid parental leave, recognition programs, an employee referral program, and dedicated time to give back (two community volunteer days and three recreational leave days each year).

Why join us?

Growth & opportunities – Build your capability, stretch into new challenges and shape what’s next with development support behind you.

Culture & connection – Be part of a team that’s inclusive, down-to-earth and genuinely connected, where collaboration is how we get things done.

Meaningful work – Do work you can feel proud of, making a real difference for customers and communities every day.

About us

As one of Australia’s largest customer-owned banks, we’re a driving force in customer owned banking, operating under the trusted brands Greater Bank and Newcastle Permanent. With over 200 years of combined experience, we’ve helped hundreds of thousands of Australians achieve home ownership and provide a genuine alternative to the big banks—all while strengthening the communities we serve. At the heart of everything we do are our core values:

Lead with heart – We genuinely care about achieving better outcomes for our customers and communities.

Own the action – We build trust by doing things the right way, with honesty and transparency.

Embrace opportunity – We look ahead, focusing on our strengths and expertise to challenge the status quo.

Thrive together – By working as one, we create the best possible future for our customers and communities.

Join a team shaping the future of trusted banking and make a real impact where it matters most.

NGM Group is committed to delivering trusted banking to enable our customers and communities to thrive. We do this by leading with heart, doing the right things in the right way, and by maintaining high standards of ethical behaviour in everything that we do. All applicants are therefore required to undergo a skills assessment, comprehensive background check, Bankruptcy Check and a Nationally Coordinated Criminal History Check.

We are also working to reflect the vibrant communities we serve. We believe in creating an inclusive, diverse, and supportive workplace where everyone can thrive. If you require adjustments to be made during the recruitment process or would like to discuss any accessibility requirements, your Talent Acquisition Partner will be happy to engage in a confidential discussion and assist. Your unique talents and perspectives are what make us stronger.

THE ROLE

Job Title: Staff Applied AI Engineer, AI Experiences & Agents
Ad Text:
The Role


The AI Agents and Experience team focused on turning frontier model capabilities into useful, trustworthy product experiences for creators, businesses, and their audiences. As an Applied AI Engineer at Linktree, you’ll work across product, design, data, and platform engineering to build AI agents and AI-powered experiences that help people create, personalize, automate, and grow from one Linktree. 

This role is for a strong product engineer who can move from concept to prototype to production. You’ll move quickly, learn from real usage, and harden what works with evaluation, observability, safeguards, and strong product judgment. This is not a pure research role. It is a build-and-ship role.

What You’ll Own

Build and ship creator-facing and visitor-facing AI experiences end-to-end, from prototype through production.
Design multi-agent workflows that combine prompting, tool use, retrieval, memory/state, orchestration, and human-in-the-loop controls where appropriate.
Create eval suites, regression harnesses, and quality metrics so AI quality improves deliberately rather than by anecdote.
Partner closely with product, design, data, and trust teams to translate ambiguous user problems into simple, high-value experiences.
Optimize latency, reliability, cost, and safety so AI features are fast, dependable, and viable at scale.
Turn production feedback, support signals, and behavioral data into model, prompt, policy, and product improvements.
Who We’re Looking For

Strong software engineering fundamentals and a track record of shipping production systems end-to-end.
Hands-on experience building LLM-powered products, agents, chat systems, RAG workflows, or similar AI experiences.
Experience building evals for AI output quality harnessing.
Strong product sense: you can identify the real user problem, simplify the solution, and make practical trade-offs around effort, scope, and quality.
Ability to frequently weigh trade-offs and understand the benefits of keeping systems architecture simple.
Good judgment on safety, trust, privacy, abuse, and failure modes in AI systems.
Strong communicator who collaborates well with cross-functional partners and explains trade-offs clearly.

Linktree is committed to providing a fair and competitive compensation package including cash, equity, and benefits. The base salary offered for this role is targeted at $240,000-$250,000 for roles based in Los Angeles. Final offers depend on multiple factors including location, experience, expertise, and role scope, and may vary from the range listed.

P.S. If you don’t tick every box in this listing, please don’t rule yourself out. We take pride in inclusion and hiring incredible human beings with great potential over ticking boxes – so if this role resonates with you, hit that apply button!

Our Story


We’re on a mission to empower anyone to curate, grow, and monetize their digital universe. We created the “link in bio” category — and we’re just getting started. From creators, artists, and entrepreneurs to global brands, we’re redefining how people connect with their communities, showcase their content and promote products — all in one link.

Today, a community of 70+ million use Linktree, including icons like Adidas, TikTok, the UN Environmental Program, The NFL, Manchester United, World Central Kitchen, Sabrina Carpenter, Olivia Rodrigo, and Selena Gomez. With 48,000 new accounts created every day, Linktree is the fastest-growing leader in our space.

We’ve teamed up with some of the world’s biggest platforms including TikTok, Snap, YouTube, GoFundMe, Spotify, Google, Stripe, Reddit, Laylo, and Kajabi, to unify Linkers’ digital spaces, and we partner with retail brands Amazon, Nike, Nordstrom, Target, Sephora, Vuori, and more to share products they love.

We’re a tight-knit, passionate team building best-in-class tools that make online presence simple, powerful, and yours. If you're excited by the idea of shaping a new category, setting the standard, and making space for everyone to be seen and succeed, we’d love to meet you.

Where and How We Work


We're a global, diverse team spread across continents with offices in London, Los Angeles, Melbourne, and San Francisco. We work together flexibly and you can choose the setup that works best for you: fully remote or a hybrid mix of office and home.

We offer autonomy in how you structure your days and weeks. While we’re mindful of time zones and occasionally collaborate outside the typical 9–5, we lean into async work to keep things flowing. Our culture is family-friendly and Zoom-casual where kids, pets, and delivery drivers are welcome.

How we’ll help you thrive


Our approach to benefits considers the whole person and the unique contributions they bring to Linktree. We want the experience at Linktree to be one that enables people to truly thrive so we can Go Further Together (one of our values). Some ways we support you:

An annual wellbeing allowance to use on things like (but not limited to) fitness memberships, development courses, childcare, travel, charitable donations, pet insurance, home office set-up - the choice is yours!
Employer contribution towards your retirement.
Generous time off for vacation, holidays, parental leave, volunteer time, and other categories.
Employee stock option program - we want each and every employee to share in the company’s success.

At Linktree, we believe in promoting a culture that celebrates unique backgrounds, talents, and experiences, and we’re proud to be an equal opportunity workplace. We are creating an inclusive workplace where every individual feels valued, respected, and has equal opportunities to thrive. We aim to foster a diverse and inclusive environment where all team members have a sense of belonging. Linktree welcomes all people regardless of sex, gender identity, race, ethnicity, disability, pregnancy, age, or other lived experience. If you require accommodations to fully participate in our opportunities, please don't hesitate to reach us at talent@linktr.ee – your needs are important to us.

Join an AI powerhouse that is going through a rapid expansion. You will help to build autonomous AI workers. This is a permanent, full-time position. It's a fully remote position if you're outside of NSW, otherwise it's approx 1 day per week in their CBD office. 
If you want to work at a company that will actually train you in AI Agent frameworks/advanced AI solutions- this is a great opportunity for you. 

In this role, you’ll lead customer-facing engagements, collaborating closely with
customers and partners technical teams, alongside the internal squad to enhance tech solutions. 

Responsibilities:

Enable enterprise customers to deploy AI agents from multiple third-party platforms (including models, LLMs, microservices and even orchestration layers) through their own secure and scalable platform.
Develop innovative AI products focusing on Node.js and integrating AI with use of various AI tools.
Discover and incorporate new partnerships, products, and technologies, including cutting-edge LLMs and AI models, from both established and emerging vendors.
Design and implement algorithms specifically for AI agents, utilizing your Node.js expertise and understanding of AI frameworks.
Strategize on integration of new features and APIs across various platforms in large-scale enterprise environments.
Requirements:

Strong background in Node.js development.
Exposure to AI toolsets OR you’re passionate about learning this (you will receive training!)
Strong experience in building secure, scalable APIs.

Experience working in a fast-paced environment
Nice to have (not essential):

AI experience
Proficiency in CICD, Kubernetes

Build a career with confidence!

From the beginning, we've led in inventing new technologies and entirely new industries. The Role

In this role, you’ll play a key part in shaping the technical excellence of our projects. You’ll produce engineering documentation, develop BMS software and graphics, optimise control systems, and provide on-site technical support to ensure every project is accurate, efficient, and fit for purpose.

Key responsibilities:

Create and update engineering drawings, points lists, functional descriptions, and O&M manuals

Develop BMS software, graphics, programming, and site-building

Tune PID loops and support commissioning teams with troubleshooting

Conduct site visits to ensure programs and graphics meet operational requirements

Configure system IP networks, including internet connectivity

Identify value-adding, cross-selling, and efficiency improvement opportunities

Communicate effectively and work collaboratively across teams

Participate in training, promotions, and cross-functional initiatives

Support a culture of continuous improvement and quality standards

Strong experience with BMS, HVAC, building controls, or automation systems

Background in managing or supporting technical teams

Experience with installations, commissioning support, and controls engineering

Well-developed analytical, reporting, and problem-solving abilities

Excellent customer service and communication skills

Highly organised, detail-oriented, and self-motivated

Able to manage multiple tasks and shifting priorities

Strong relationship-building ability and a solutions mindset

Benefits:

Attractive salary package + Full time permanent opportunity

Car allowance + mobile phone + laptop

Excellent job stability + Ongoing training and development opportunities

Inclusive work environment + Supportive management team

Our commitment to you

Join us and make a difference.

Apply Now!

Job Expectations


Build your career and Australia’s future.

Not many people can say they are working on building Australia’s future. With us you’ll be doing just that, leaving a legacy for all Australians. Plus, there’s equal employment, great training, and true flexible working arrangements.

We have an exciting opportunity at nbn, as the AI and Automation Lead, reporting to the Program Manager for CX and Digital Transformation.

A bit about your role

FW Network Delivery is focused on strengthening digital capability to enable smarter, faster, and more reliable network operations through customer‑led design, simplified workflows, and effective digital transformation.

The digital transformation function delivers improved customer and employee experiences by providing innovative digital solutions, operational excellence, and robust engineering frameworks as a shared service across the organisation.

The AI & Automation also lead drives the automation and AI transformation by designing and running solutions that combine process automation, AI, and data‑driven engineering.

Finally, the role ensures initiatives deliver measurable business value while embedding continuous improvement and long‑term capability uplift across FW Network Delivery.

A bit about you

The role requires extensive experience delivering large‑scale, complex solutions in telecommunications or technology environments.

It demands strong leadership capability with proven experience managing technical teams, projects, and vendors across agile and waterfall delivery models.

The candidate must also have deep expertise in enterprise and solution architecture, including creating and maintaining architecture artefacts, applying standards and frameworks, and leveraging modern technologies such as automation platforms, AI, ML, and API‑driven orchestration.

Strong stakeholder management, communication, and influencing skills are also essential.

Life at nbn

To be part of nbn is to be part of something bigger. There’s so much more from here. Our pioneering spirit drives us forward every day. Together, we help lift the digital capability of the nation by building and connecting Australia’s best fibre infrastructure into homes and businesses, plus places and things, everywhere. We’re continuing to create Australia’s network—a network made for more. Not just a way for everyone to do the things they love but transforming the way they do them.

A more inclusive working world

nbn is committed to an inclusive, flexible, and supportive workplace where everyone can feel heard, valued, and respected. We believe diverse perspectives are the key to unlocking greater outcomes and encourage applications from people of all ages, nationalities, abilities, and cultures. That's why we have a Reconciliation Action Plan, Accessibility & Inclusion Plan, offer 18 weeks of paid parental leave, are a Pride in Diversity Platinum Employer, and have active employee-led diversity pillars. Click here to see our list of employee benefits and why we're recognised as a WORK180 Endorsed Employer for Women.

Equal Opportunities for All

For other matters, please visit our Contact Us page on our website.

Where to from here?

Please note that this is a permanent opportunity and applications for this role will close at midnight, Thursday 9th of April 2026.

To be eligible for this role, you must have full working rights in Australia.

The Role:
Are you passionate about turning data into a real-world impact? Join us as a Senior Data Engineer and help lead a new wave of analytics innovation. You’ll be a key part of a dedicated project team delivering high-value, MVP-driven solutions. This role is responsible for advancing our data science practices, from model development to deployment and governance. 

What’s in it for you? 

Build and deploy statistical models and advanced analytics to solve real business challenges
Influence and uplift our end-to-end data science capability
Work with modern Azure-based platforms
Collaborate with talented teams across analytics, business intelligence, actuarial, data engineering, and business departments
Be part of a values-led organisation with a strong purpose and community focus
Flexible hybrid work arrangement with work from home and in-office
Skills & Experience:

5–7 years’ experience as a Data Engineer / Analytics Engineer in a commercial setting.
Bachelor’s or higher degree in computer science, information systems, data/analytics engineering, or a related quantitative field.
Proven ability to design, build, and productionise scalable ELT pipelines and analytics datasets (batch and streaming) using modern tooling.
Experience maturing data platforms and engineering practices, including standards for modelling, testing, code review, CI/CD, and governance.
Expertise with Google Cloud data stack—e.g. BigQuery, Cloud Storage, Dataform or dbt (for ELT), Cloud Composer (Airflow), Dataflow/Beam, Pub/Sub, Cloud Run—and strong familiarity with Looker semantic modelling (LookML).
Experience with Azure platforms is desirable. (Data Factories, Fabric Services)
Experience with Microsoft Power BI reports and generating on-demand reports. 
Proficient with modern developer workflows: Git, GitHub/GitLab/Cloud Build CI/CD, Infrastructure-as-Code (e.g., Terraform), notebooks where appropriate.
Strong SQL (performance tuning, partitioning/clustering) and dimensional/data-vault modelling skills.
Highly proficient in Python for data engineering (packaging, testing, virtual environments); familiarity with R an advantage where needed for analytics handoffs.
Solid understanding of security, cost optimisation, observability (logging/metrics/alerts), and data quality frameworks (tests, CI checks, SLAs/SLIs).
Highly Desirable  

Experience in Insurance or Banking 
Power BI skills 
Microsoft Azure Certifications 
The Company:
Established since 1953 offering stability and security, a great place to grow and develop your career. Having a reputation for excellence you will be able to utilise your skills on challenging and meaningful projects, supported by other subject matter experts within our own collaborative and supportive team. 
You will be joining a team from various walks of life accompanied by a broad range of skillsets in this highly collaborative environment where teamwork is our most highly valued asset.
  
It is essential that you demonstrate in your career, proven experience in your field of expertise. We need forward thinkers, individuals to provide substantiated innovative thinking and solutions to the business.

Please note: A criminal background check and written assessment will make up part of the recruitment process.

We are partnering with a leading AI software company focused on building enterprise-grade Generative AI and machine learning solutions for large-scale organisations across APAC and globally.

This organisation is at the forefront of combining Generative AI, predictive modelling, and agentic systems to help enterprises deploy secure, production-ready AI applications on their own data environments. Their technology is widely adopted across Fortune 500 companies and regulated industries, with strong enterprise traction across financial services, healthcare, government, and large-scale commercial sectors.

About the Opportunity

We are seeking a Senior AI Engineer to join a high-performing, customer-facing AI engineering team. This is a hands-on role focused on designing, building, and deploying end-to-end AI solutions that move beyond experimentation into real production environments.

You will work on advanced Generative AI systems including LLM applications, agent-based architectures, and scalable ML pipelines. This role sits close to customers, meaning your work will directly influence enterprise deployments and real-world business outcomes from day one.

You will collaborate with highly experienced ML engineers, data scientists, and AI specialists to deliver robust, production-ready AI systems.

This role is based in Sydney and involves close collaboration with enterprise customers across APAC.

Key Responsibilities
AI & LLM Engineering

Design and build agentic AI systems to automate complex enterprise workflows

Develop LLM-based applications using RAG, prompt engineering, fine-tuning, and tool/function calling

Implement guardrails, evaluation frameworks, and responsible AI controls for production systems

Stay current with emerging GenAI frameworks and agentic architectures and apply them in real-world deployments

End-to-End AI Delivery

Own full lifecycle delivery of AI solutions from problem definition through to production deployment

Build scalable APIs and backend services to expose AI capabilities to enterprise applications

Deploy models across cloud, on-prem, and hybrid environments

Develop ML pipelines and LLMOps frameworks for monitoring, iteration, and continuous improvement

Customer-Facing Delivery

Work directly with enterprise stakeholders to translate business problems into AI solutions

Support pre-sales activities including proof-of-concept development and technical demonstrations

Communicate effectively across technical and non-technical audiences

Collaborate with cross-functional teams to deliver high-quality, production-ready solutions

What We’re Looking For
Experience

3+ years’ experience in AI/ML engineering or applied machine learning roles

Strong experience building LLM-based applications (RAG, agents, fine-tuning, etc.)

Strong Python engineering capability and experience with ML frameworks (e.g. PyTorch, TensorFlow, scikit-learn)

Experience deploying AI systems in cloud or enterprise environments (AWS, Azure, GCP, Kubernetes)

Technical Skills

Strong understanding of modern GenAI concepts: RAG, prompt engineering, evaluation, guardrails, LLMOps

Solid foundation in classical ML and model selection

Backend engineering experience including REST APIs, containers (Docker/Kubernetes), and CI/CD

Ability to build production-grade, scalable AI systems

Soft Skills

Strong problem-solving ability and comfort working in ambiguous environments

Ability to communicate complex technical concepts clearly to stakeholders

Customer-facing experience or ability to work in pre-sales / consulting-style environments is highly valued

Nice to Have

Competitive ML background

Experience in regulated industries such as financial services or healthcare

Exposure to enterprise ML platforms or AutoML systems

Experience in field engineering, solution engineering, or customer-facing technical roles

Why This Role?

Opportunity to work on cutting-edge Generative AI and agentic systems

Customer-facing role with direct enterprise impact

High-calibre technical environment with strong ML engineering talent

Exposure to complex real-world AI problems at scale

Strong career growth within a rapidly evolving AI space

Engineering - Software (Information & Communication Technology)
Government - Federal (Government & Defence)
Ad Text:
The Australian Communications and Media Authority (ACMA) is an independent Commonwealth statutory authority responsible for the regulation of broadcasting, radiocommunications, telecommunications and some online content.

This work underpins our vision for a connected, informed and entertained Australia.

Who we are

The Corporate and Research Division partners with business areas across the ACMA, to provide enterprise wide enabling services and strategic advice so that the ACMA can achieve its purpose. The Division facilitates this through the delivery of key corporate functions including financial management, property services, security, procurement advice, performance reporting, research and data, digital and technology services, human resources, communications, and governance services.

The Research, Data and Governance Branch is at the forefront of shaping and delivering impactful consumer and market research that drives regulatory decisions and supports the ACMA’s compliance and enforcement priorities. We champion the ACMA’s data strategy and governance framework, ensuring agency decision-making is supported by high-quality data, sharp analytics, and solid evidence-based insights. The Branch also provides essential governance services, robust risk and fraud management frameworks, seamless parliamentary coordination, efficient internal audit contract management, and targeted international advice and services. The Branch supports the Audit and Risk Committee with secretariat services and oversees the ACMA’s strategic planning and performance reporting.

The Data Platform Services Section is responsible for designing, delivering, and maintaining a modern, scalable enterprise-wide data platform that supports ACMA’s analytical and operational need. Our focus is ensuring that the platform is optimised to meet evolving business requirements and enabling advanced capabilities such as self-service analytics and data science.

The team plays a lead role in enhancing data platform architecture and tooling to support robust analytics, governance, building and maintaining data pipelines along with proactively monitoring and optimising platform for performance and reliability.

We ensure that the ACMA’s data platform is ready to take full advantage of new technical developments and opportunities.

About the role

If you’re a Data Engineer passionate about modern data platforms and emerging technologies, and eager to contribute to initiatives across data engineering, Machine Learning and data science, we’d love to hear from you.

As a Principal Data Engineer, you will operate beyond traditional data engineering by owning the architectural direction, technical leadership, and strategic uplift of our cloud‑based data platform. You will play a pivotal role in enabling advanced analytics, data science, and AI capabilities, while leading the ongoing modernisation and evolution of our data and analytics landscape to support emerging technologies, scalable patterns and future business needs.

This role will involve working within a highly collaborative team, with opportunities to grow your expertise in modern data tech stack and expand your knowledge with hands on experience with latest data engineering and cloud technologies.

Key Duties

In this role you will:

Own the end‑to‑end design, architecture, and technical governance of Azure-based enterprise data platforms, ensuring scalability, resilience, and alignment with long‑term organisational strategy
Define and champion architectural standards, data models, integration patterns, and engineering best practices across teams, influencing platform evolution and engineering direction
Provide senior technical leadership by collaborating with Section managers, Data and Solution Architects, Analysts, Engineers, and Executive stakeholders to define platform direction, influence architectural decisions, and ensure solutions align with long‑term data strategy
Act as a technical advisor, partnering with architects, managers, SMEs, and business stakeholders to ensure data solutions meet enterprise architecture and strategic outcomes
Lead research and development of proof‑of‑concepts for emerging data technologies, ingestion patterns and modern architectures, driving innovation and contributing directly to ACMA data strategy outlined in the Data strategy 2025
Create, maintain, and govern technical documentation and standards, including enterprise integration patterns, architecture blueprints, platform reference models, best‑practice engineering guidelines
Mentor Senior and Mid‑level Data Engineers, providing technical coaching, setting engineering standards, and developing the organisation’s data engineering capability
Oversee and guide the delivery, deployment, and operationalisation of new data products and platform capabilities, ensuring production readiness and adherence to best practices.

To be eligible for employment with ACMA, applicants must be an Australian citizen.

The successful candidate will also be required to obtain and maintain a Baseline level security clearance.
